MOTION To Compel Further, Verified Responses To Discovery And Award Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-26-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Andrea Casalett, Esq., for defendant and moving party: ITT Corporation
Jamil White, Esq., and Bruce Jackson, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Albert Wright

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant ITT Corporation's motion to compel further responses to interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted. The motion is not moot as the supplemental responses are unverified. The Court finds that plaintiff initially responded with blanket objections, failed to make a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the matters in dispute by failing to respond to defendant's meet and confer letters of July 18 and August 21, 2008 and asserted general objections that are not authorized by the Code of Civil Procedure. Plaintiff is ordered to serve verified supplemental responses within 15 days of notice of this order. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,040 shall be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Commissioner: Bruce Chan; Court Reporter: Deborah Tageart, CSR # 5942
